npcs.name: Marcus Thorne
npcs.reputation: The most visible Ventrue working to shape succession. Known for strict adherence to tradition and absolute rejection of compromise. Respected by traditionalists, feared by pragmatists, hated by Anarchs.
npcs.story_function: Primary point of contact for Ventrue succession efforts. Can provide access to Camarilla power structures, but demands absolute loyalty and adherence to tradition. Represents the hardline Ventrue approach to restoring order.

npcs.name: Victoria Sterling
npcs.reputation: Uses Status, social pressure, and legal leverage to enforce Ventrue authority. Never gets her hands dirty—others do the violence while she handles the paperwork. Feared for her ability to destroy reputations and revoke domains through proper channels.
npcs.story_function: Represents Ventrue's bureaucratic approach to enforcement. Can provide information about social networks, Status hierarchies, and legal frameworks. Can also use her influence to help or harm PCs depending on their actions.

npcs.name: Robert Chen
npcs.reputation: The face of pragmatist Ventrue. Willing to negotiate with Giovanni, make limited deals with Anarchs, and adapt traditions to fit modern circumstances. Respected by pragmatists, viewed as traitor by traditionalists.
npcs.story_function: Can facilitate negotiations between hostile factions. Represents the possibility of compromise and adaptation. Useful for PCs who want to prevent violence or find middle-ground solutions. Creates internal Ventrue conflict.

npcs.name: Eleanor Blackwood
npcs.reputation: Secretly negotiating with Giovanni for resources and support. Her compromise makes her vulnerable to blackmail and manipulation. Respected by pragmatists who don't know the full extent of her dealings, hated by traditionalists who suspect her.
npcs.story_function: Represents the dangerous compromises pragmatists make. Can provide access to Giovanni resources, but at a cost. Her secret dealings create opportunities for manipulation and betrayal. PCs might discover her compromise and use it, or be caught in the fallout when it's exposed.

npcs.name: Alexander Cross
npcs.reputation: Uses economic pressure to enforce Ventrue authority. Controls resources, cuts off funding, makes resistance expensive. Respected for his effectiveness, feared for his ruthlessness, hated by those he's economically crushed.
npcs.story_function: Represents Ventrue's economic approach to control. Can provide or deny resources, funding, and business connections. Useful for PCs who need economic support, dangerous for PCs who oppose Ventrue authority. Shows how Ventrue use money as a weapon.

npcs.name: Patricia Montclair
npcs.reputation: Maintains legal frameworks that justify Ventrue actions. Documents everything, creates precedents, uses law to enforce authority. Respected for her knowledge, feared for her ability to make anything legal or illegal through proper channels.
npcs.story_function: Represents Ventrue's legal approach to control. Can provide information about legal frameworks, precedents, and proper channels. Can help PCs navigate Ventrue bureaucracy or use legal leverage against them. Shows how Ventrue use law as a weapon.

npcs.name: James Whitmore
npcs.reputation: Represents old-world Ventrue authority. Clings to tradition, rejects all compromise, believes in absolute hierarchy. Respected by traditionalists, viewed as outdated by pragmatists, hated by Anarchs.
npcs.story_function: Represents Ventrue traditionalism and the weight of history. Can provide access to ancient traditions, clan history, and old-world authority. Creates conflict with pragmatists and represents the tension between tradition and adaptation.

npcs.name: Sarah Mitchell
npcs.reputation: Young Ventrue trying to establish domain and prove herself. Caught between pragmatist and traditionalist factions, trying to navigate internal politics while building her own power base. Respected for ambition, viewed as potential threat by elders.
npcs.story_function: Represents the next generation of Ventrue and the internal competition for power. Can provide insight into Ventrue internal politics and succession struggles. Useful for PCs who want to understand or manipulate Ventrue hierarchy. Shows how ambition creates conflict within the clan.
